commands.review_summary:
This PR changes WhatsApp Web logout cleanup to rename managed auth directories to timestamped `.bak` directories and updates logout/auth-store tests to expect backups.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +29. Total +67 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. not for the original plugin-update report: the issue gives concrete steps, but this review did not run a real WhatsApp update. The PR-introduced behavior is source-reproducible because it renames the active auth directory while current readers only check active `creds.json`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer review and contributor rework because the fix must separate plugin-update preservation from explicit logout/security semantics, and the external PR lacks real WhatsApp update proof.

target.title: [Bug]: `openclaw plugins update whatsapp` silently wipes Baileys session — full QR re-pair required after every minor plugin update
target.body:
## Summary
fix(whatsapp): backup credentials instead of deleting on logout/update

When `openclaw plugins update whatsapp` runs, the plugin's logout procedure silently deletes the Baileys session credentials directory, forcing a full QR re-pair after every minor update. This changes `logoutWeb()` to rename the auth directory to a timestamped backup path (`.bak.<timestamp>`) instead of deleting it, so existing sessions are preserved. Falls back to `fs.rm()` if the rename fails.

Fixes #92546

## Behavior addressed
Changed `logoutWeb()` in WhatsApp auth-store to rename the auth directory to `<dir>.bak.<timestamp>` instead of deleting it recursively. This preserves the Baileys session credentials so `openclaw plugins update whatsapp` doesn't force a full QR re-pair.

## Real behavior proof

### behavior
Changed `logoutWeb()` in WhatsApp auth-store to rename the auth directory to `<dir>.bak.<timestamp>` instead of deleting it recursively. This preserves the Baileys session credentials so `openclaw plugins update whatsapp` doesn't force a full QR re-pair.

### environment
- **Setup**: Local dev environment with WhatsApp plugin, pnpm workspace, Vitest
- **Runtime**: node

### steps
```bash
# Run the WhatsApp auth-store tests
cd extensions/whatsapp
pnpm test -- --run src/auth-store.test.ts
pnpm test -- --run src/logout.test.ts
```

### observedResult
```
✓ auth-store > clearBaileysAuthFiles > should create a backup when auth dir exists (58 ms)
✓ web logout > backups credentials instead of deleting when present (78 ms)
✓ web logout > backs up oauth.json too when not using legacy auth dir (62 ms)
  … all 24 tests passing
```

## What was not tested
E2E tests with a real WhatsApp Web session. Manual verification of credential recovery from backup.
